old_claim.note: Instrument Calibration Standards provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: Assay results depended on weighing sampled material and the recovered precious-metal bead, making balance-scale practice an enabling measurement capability.

source_shape.source_locator: The Fire Assaying Today section describes mixing a known amount of carefully sampled powdered rock, weighing the gold and silver bead, and reweighing after parting.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source makes weighing an explicit part of the fire-assay workflow before and after separating precious metals.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Because the corrected node is anchored to 1556, a 1614 instrument-calibration node is chronologically wrong; earlier balance-scale practice captures the measurement step without importing later scientific-instrument standardization.

ontology_before: Chemical Assay Laboratories depended on 1614 Instrument Calibration Standards, which was same-year placeholder infrastructure and becomes later than the 1556 assay anchor.
ontology_after: Metallurgical Fire Assay Workshops depend on earlier balance-scale practice for weighing sampled ore and recovered beads.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: chemical_assay_laboratories now depends on portable_scales and no longer directly depends on instrument_calibration_standards.
would_reject_if: The node were redated back to a post-1614 laboratory standardization scope.
A source showed early fire assay did not require weighing samples or recovered metal beads.
A narrower early laboratory-balance node were introduced and should replace portable_scales.
short_reason: Fire assay needs weighing, while the previous calibration-standard edge is later than the 1556 anchor.
